title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE BOBON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOLâANNEX B IN BARANGAY DAHICAN, CITY OF MATI, PROVINCE OF DAVAO ORIENTAL FROM THE BOBON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS DOÃA ROSA GARCIA RABAT MEMORIAL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
108 OG No. 50, 6320 (December 10, 2012)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10213, October 18, 2012 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE BOBON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOLâANNEX B IN BARANGAY DAHICAN, CITY OF MATI, PROVINCE OF DAVAO ORIENTAL FROM THE BOBON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS DOÃA ROSA GARCIA RABAT MEMORIAL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School. â

The Bobon National High School â Annex B in Barangay Dahican, City of Mati, Province of Davao Oriental is hereby separated from the Bobon National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Doña Rosa Garcia Rabat Memorial National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

â All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Bobon National High School â Annex B in Barangay Dahican are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Dona Rosa Garcia Rabat Memorial National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations. â

The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Departmentâs program the operationalization of the Doña Rosa Garcia Rabat Memorial National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current yearâs appropriations of the Bobon National High School â Annex B in Barangay Dahican. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation. â

The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

â This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on August 3, 2011 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

OCT 18 2012

(Sgd.) BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
